question_text: Which statement correctly explains what happens to an object in free fall?

options.label: A
options.text: The object falls at a constant speed because gravity pulls with constant force.

options.label: B
options.text: The object accelerates downwards because the only force acting on it is gravity (ignoring air resistance).

options.label: C
options.text: The object's weight decreases as it falls towards Earth.

options.label: D
options.text: The object falls faster on Earth than on the Moon because Earth's gravity is stronger, but the acceleration is different.

explanation: In free fall, gravity is the only force acting on an object, so it accelerates downwards at 10 m/s² on Earth. This means it gets faster and faster, not at constant speed. The weight doesn't change during the fall—it's the gravitational force pulling the object down.
